questions: What is Lofi?
answer: Lofi is a platform designed to supercharge your online advertising efforts by making campaign creation and management simple and effective.

questions: What can I do in the Lofi app?
answer:
Lofi helps you build, launch, and manage ads efficiently across channels.

Dashboard – Your home base for performance overviews with interactive panels (Conversion Performance, Spend Effectiveness) and quick date/campaign filters.
Campaigns – Manage campaigns in List or Timeline view. Create, clone, pause, or review drafts.
Creative – Asset library and AI studio. Upload images/videos, launch Adobe Express, generate copy with AI, and preview placements.
Help – Search the knowledge base, browse Common Topics, or click Ask Lucy for AI guidance.
Settings – Configure account, billing, brand profile, ad account connections, and user management.

questions: How easy is the platform to use?
answer: Lofi is designed to be intuitive and user-friendly, guiding you through campaign creation with built-in best practices.

questions: How can I contact Lofi?
answer: Reach us via the website, support chat, or email support@meetlofi.com.

questions: How can I schedule a demonstration of the Lofi platform?
answer: Contact Lofi through support channels to schedule a demo.

questions: What types of campaigns am I prohibited from advertising with Lofi?
answer: Lofi follows the advertising standards of Meta, Google, TikTok, and other platforms. Refer to each platform's policy for details.

questions: What is Lucy’s pre-launch audit?
answer: Lucy checks your campaign setup before launch to catch missing inputs or errors and ensure quality before your ad goes live.

questions: Where are the video tutorials?
answer: In the Help section under ‘Video Tutorials.’ Learn how to build, optimize, and scale campaigns step-by-step.

questions: What is the Getting Started Checklist?
answer: A step-by-step list to launch your first campaign: connect ad accounts, install conversion tracking, add billing, upload brand assets, and create your initial campaign with Lofi’s four-step wizard.

questions: How do I access Lucy and the Help Center?
answer: Click Help in the left menu or use the Help page search bar. For instant AI assistance, click Ask Lucy in the Help header.

questions: How does billing work with Lofi?
answer: Billing cycles are monthly. Manage payment methods and view/download invoices under Settings > Billing.

questions: How do I cancel my account?
answer: Go to Billing > Change Plan > Cancel Plan, or email support@meetlofi.com.

questions: What do you get on the Core Plan vs. the Advanced Plan?
answer: Plans offer different features and benefits. See plan details in-app for a full comparison.

questions: How much does Lofi cost?
answer:
Core Plan: $500/month + ad spend + 12% service fee.

Advanced Plan: $950/month + ad spend + 10% service fee.

questions: What is the service fee for?
answer: The service fee covers use of the platform, automation tools, AI modeling, and continuous optimization features.

questions: How can I prevent being automatically recharged after my ad spend credits run out?
answer: Manage automatic recharges in Billing settings or contact support for help.

questions: Can I update my payment method and billing info?
answer: Yes. Under Settings > Billing, click ‘Update Payment Method’ or ‘Update Billing Info.’

questions: Where can I find past invoices?
answer: View and download recent invoices at the bottom of the Billing section.

questions: Can I change my plan?
answer: Yes. Click ‘Upgrade Plan’ or ‘View Plan Options’ in Settings > Billing.

questions: How many people can I invite to my organization?
answer: There is no limit to the number of people you can invite to your organization.

questions: How do I get started with my first campaign?
answer: Click ‘Create Campaign’ and follow the four-step flow: choose goal (Conversions, Traffic, Awareness) → select platforms and ad formats → add creative assets and copy → review Lucy’s audit and Launch.

questions: What campaign goals can I choose?
answer: Conversions, Traffic, or Awareness.

questions: Who can I target with my ads?
answer: New Customers, Existing Customers, or Website Visitors. You can specify geographic regions with radius-based targeting. Expert users can also set age range, gender, and interests.

questions: What is the difference between Standard and Evergreen campaigns?
answer: Standard: run between specific start/end dates. Evergreen: run continuously until paused or stopped.

questions: What platforms can I advertise on?
answer: Lofi supports Social (e.g., Facebook, Instagram, TikTok), Search (e.g., Google), Display, CTV, and more. Explore supported channels in campaign creation.

questions: Can I preview my ad before launching?
answer: Yes. The preview panel shows how your ad will look on selected platforms such as Facebook and Google.

questions: How do I use the campaign timeline view?
answer: Switch to the ‘Timeline’ tab under Campaigns to view campaigns on a calendar for scheduling and overlap tracking.

questions: Can I filter campaigns by platform?
answer: Yes. Use the platform filter to sort campaigns by channel.

questions: What does the campaign status ‘Scheduled’ mean?
answer: The campaign has a future start date. It will begin once that date is reached and the platform has approved creative and targeting elements.

questions: How do I save a draft or resume campaign creation later?
answer: In the Create Campaign flow, click ‘Save as Draft’ (top right). Resume under Campaigns > Drafts.

questions: What are best practices for budgeting?
answer: Use the 70-20-10 approach: 70% to proven campaigns, 20% to new tests, 10% to experimental channels. Adjust monthly based on performance.

questions: How do I clone a campaign or work from a draft?
answer: In Campaigns, click the ‘…’ menu next to a live or draft campaign and choose Duplicate, then edit the copy.

questions: What tools can I use to create or edit visuals in Lofi?
answer: Use ‘Create with Adobe Express’ to generate and edit images, apply brand templates, or design ad visuals from scratch.

questions: Can I generate ad copy and creative with AI?
answer: Yes. Use ‘Generate with AI’ for copy and Adobe Express for AI-generated visuals.

questions: Can I test different creatives?
answer: Yes. Upload multiple creatives in the same campaign. Lofi will automatically A/B test and surface top performers.

questions: Where do I find Brand Guidelines?
answer: Go to Settings > Brand to set your logo, color palette, fonts, messaging pillars, and tone rules.

questions: Do brand settings affect creative output?
answer: Yes. Your brand settings are reflected in AI-generated assets and creative templates across the platform.

questions: How do I manage and organize my creative assets?
answer: Open Creative to upload images, videos, and audio. Use search, sort (newest, oldest, name), and filters (type, usage) to organize.

questions: How does the Adobe Express integration work?
answer: Click ‘Create with Adobe Express’ in the Creative header to launch the studio, choose templates, apply your brand, and export directly to your asset library.

questions: What is auto-rotation of assets?
answer: When multiple creatives are uploaded, Lofi automatically rotates and tests them. Underperformers are phased out and top performers are prioritized.

questions: How does Lofi ensure brand consistency across my ads?
answer: Your Settings > Brand profile (logo, colors, fonts, tone) is applied to AI-generated assets and templates for consistent creative output.

questions: What are Ad Campaign Best Practices?
answer: Use mobile-first formats (9:16 Stories, 1:1 feeds), front-load benefits in headlines, apply your brand profile, and let Lofi auto-rotate assets to surface top performers. For details, see the best practice guide.

questions: Can I filter dashboard data by campaign or date range?
answer: Yes. Use the dropdowns to filter by date and select specific campaigns for comparison.

questions: What do the metrics in Spend Effectiveness and Conversion Performance mean?
answer:
Impressions: How many times your ad displayed.
CTR (Click-Through Rate) = (Clicks ÷ Impressions) × 100%: Indicates ad relevance.
Clicks: Number of taps/clicks; shows interest.
Conversion Rate = (Conversions ÷ Clicks) × 100%: Effectiveness of turning clicks into actions.
Conversions: Count of tracked goals (purchases, sign-ups).
Conversion Value: Sum of revenue/value from conversions.
Ad Spend: Total dollars spent in the period.
CPM: Cost to show your ad 1,000 times.
CPC: Cost for each click.
CPA (Cost per Conversion) = (Ad Spend ÷ Conversions): Efficiency for desired actions.
ROAS = (Conversion Value ÷ Ad Spend): Revenue per dollar spent.

questions: Why are my dashboard stats showing zero?
answer: Your campaign may not be live yet, filters may exclude data, or no spend occurred in the selected date range.

questions: How do I compare performance across different date ranges?
answer: On the Dashboard, open the date picker and select presets (Last 7 days, Last 3 months) or a Custom Range. Charts and metrics update instantly.

questions: How do I export my performance data?
answer: Click Export in the top-right of any dashboard widget to download a CSV or PDF of the displayed metrics.

questions: How do I create an account?
answer: Visit the Lofi platform and follow the sign-up process.

questions: How do I reset a lost or forgotten password?
answer: Use the ‘Forgot Password’ link on the login page to reset your password.

questions: How do I update my email address or password?
answer: Go to Settings > Account, then click Change Email or Change Password.

questions: Can I enable two-factor authentication?
answer: Yes. Under Settings > Account, open Security Settings and enable Two-Factor Authentication.

questions: Where can I see my login history?
answer: Settings > Account > Login History.

questions: What roles can I assign to team members?
answer:
Admin: Full access to everything.
Contributor: Can build campaigns.
Viewer: View-only access.
Reviewer (Coming Soon): Review ads only.

questions: How do I invite team members?
answer: Go to Settings > User Management and click ‘Invite User.’

questions: Can I change someone’s role later?
answer: Yes. Click the ‘…’ next to the user’s name and adjust their role.

questions: How do I define brand guidelines in Lofi?
answer: Go to Settings > Brand. Specify brand strategy, upload visuals (e.g., logo), and define tone, voice, and messaging.

questions: What is the AI Brand Scanner?
answer: Lucy scans your website and auto-fills your brand information to save time and enforce visual consistency.

questions: How do I connect ad accounts?
answer: Go to Settings > Ad Accounts and click ‘Connect’ next to the platform you want to add.

questions: Can I request a new integration?
answer: Use the 'Contact Us for Integration Needs' button in the Integrations section.

questions: How do I create a campaign end-to-end?
answer: Create a campaign in four guided steps: (1) Define goal, objective, dates, budget. (2) Select platforms, formats, and targeting (audiences, locations, age, gender, interests). (3) Add ad details and assets; use AI to generate or scan your site; run compliance checks. (4) Review toggles (creative, audience, budget optimization, automated reporting), then submit. If scheduled, complete payment or set evergreen to go LIVE on start date.

questions: Which campaign type/platform should I choose for best ROAS?
answer: Pick based on goal and funnel stage: Search for high intent and direct response; Social for prospecting and retargeting with visual assets; Display for broad, efficient reach; YouTube/CTV for scalable awareness with measurable lift; Audio for incremental reach and brand recall. Lucy can recommend a mix given your budget, product, and target.

questions: How should I define my target audience?
answer: Define audiences by locations, age ranges, gender, and interests. Optionally upload customer documents (existing or website-derived) and add a plain-language target description. Lucy converts these into actionable segments and tests lookalike/interest clusters for ROAS lift.

questions: How do I set budget and schedule for ROAS?
answer: Set budget and flight dates in Step 1. One-time budgets fund a fixed flight. Evergreen ties to a monthly subscription (Stripe) and auto-extends, letting Lucy optimize continuously. For ROAS, start with conservative daily caps, then reallocate to best performers weekly.

questions: How do I add or create ad assets?
answer: Upload images/video or select from your library. Use Adobe Express directly from Lofi to create on-brand creatives. The platform filters assets by acceptable formats for your selected placements and tags usage automatically. Lucy can suggest creative specs per platform to drive ROAS.

questions: How can Lucy generate ad copy and creatives?
answer: Use AI Ad Suggestions in Step 3 for headlines, primary text, and CTAs. Use Scan Website to extract product name, description, and target audience from your URL. Combine AI-generated copy with on-brand assets. Lucy prioritizes variants likely to improve CTR and conversion rate for higher ROAS.

questions: How do I ensure my ads are compliant?
answer: Run compliance checks in Step 3. Lofi uses AI to evaluate creative, industry-specific, platform, and state-specific rules, then returns a consolidated report. Fix flagged items before launch to avoid disapprovals and wasted spend.

questions: What do campaign statuses mean and how do they change?
answer: Statuses: DRAFT → SCHEDULED → LIVE → COMPLETED. You can PAUSE/RESUME while LIVE/PAUSED. Drafts and Scheduled can be edited; unpaid Scheduled campaigns show a Pay action. Completed campaigns can be Relaunched. Status updates auto-advance based on start/end dates and payment state.

questions: How do I pay for a campaign?
answer: For one-time flights, Scheduled campaigns show a Pay action that opens Stripe checkout. For Evergreen, subscribe to a monthly plan and Lofi tracks spend per active months. Manage payment methods, billing address, and download invoices via the Customer Portal.

questions: How do I connect Google Ads?
answer: Admins can authorize Google Ads access and store OAuth tokens. You can then list and link Google Ads and Lofi campaigns for unified tracking and governance. This enables metrics aggregation and simplifies cross-platform operations.

questions: How do I track performance and ROAS?
answer: Use the Dashboard to view clicks, impressions, conversions, cost, conversion value, CTR, CPM, CPC, cost per conversion, conversion rate, and ROAS. Filter by date range and campaigns. Lucy flags underperformers and suggests reallocations to improve ROAS.

questions: How do I manage assets and reuse creatives?
answer: The library stores all uploaded and Adobe-created assets. Lofi tags campaign usage in storage metadata so you can find used vs. unused content and avoid duplicates. Mark favorites for quick access and filter by type/usage when assembling new campaigns.

questions: How do brand settings affect creative?
answer: Use Brand settings to store logos, colors, and tone preferences. Lucy uses these to suggest on-brand copy and creative. Standardize brand inputs so AI suggestions and Adobe Express designs stay consistent across campaigns.

questions: What integrations are available?
answer: The Integrations page lists common tools (e.g., GA, Mailchimp). Some connections are in-progress or available for early access. Use the contact dialog for integrations not yet enabled and Lucy will guide interim workflows.

questions: How do I edit an existing campaign?
answer: Edit Draft, Scheduled, Live, or Paused campaigns to adjust name, goal, targeting, platforms, ad details, assets, dates, and budget. Status change timestamps are tracked for history. For Completed campaigns, use Relaunch to create a new flight with prior settings.

questions: What happens on the campaign start and end dates?
answer: On the start date, paid Scheduled campaigns move to LIVE automatically. On the day after the end date, LIVE campaigns without subscriptions move to COMPLETED and stop spending. Timestamps are recorded and can be monitored in the campaign list and dashboard.

questions: How do I get help or report an issue?
answer: Use the Help section for product guidance and search. Lucy answers marketing questions directly and can switch to detailed mode for trend and competitor research when needed. For account-specific issues, use Claims or contact support.

questions: What does Lofi not support in the broader digital marketing stack?
answer: Lofi focuses specifically on paid advertising and does not support: Landing page creation/hosting (no native builder or optimization tools), SEO optimization (no keyword ranking, backlink tracking, or site audits), Content marketing (no blogging, long-form content, or organic social scheduling), Email/SMS marketing (no campaign design, list management, or automation), Marketing automation/CRM (no nurture flows, scoring, or CRM integration), Influencer/affiliate management (no workflows for sourcing or tracking external partners), Organic social media scheduling (focus is only on paid ads), or Customer data platform (CDP) features (no single-customer view or deep segmentation).

questions: What does Lofi support for paid advertising?
answer: Lofi supports multi-channel paid advertising across Meta, Google, TikTok, LinkedIn, YouTube, and other platforms. Features include AI-assisted ad copywriting and creative generation (images and videos), creative previews and variations guided by Lucy's agents, customer and competitor research with market benchmarks, automated performance analytics and cross-channel insights, brand settings and compliance enforcement, multi-user collaboration with role-based permissions, and centralized brand management with cross-platform ad account integrations.

questions: What can Lucy's AI agents do for my campaigns?
answer: Lucy's specialized agents provide comprehensive support: Creative Director Agent guides creative previews and variations, Copywriting Agent assists with AI-assisted ad copywriting, Campaign Planning Agent helps with customer and competitor research plus market benchmarks, Performance Analyst Agent delivers automated analytics and optimization recommendations, and Compliance Agent ensures brand guidelines, keyword blacklists, CTA alignment, and platform/industry compliance checks.

questions: How does team collaboration work in Lofi?
answer: Lofi supports multi-user collaboration with role-based permissions: Admin (full access), Contributor (can create campaigns), Reviewer (approves ads - coming soon), and Viewer (read-only access). Teams can work together on centralized brand management, cross-platform ad account integrations, and shared campaign workflows with appropriate access controls for different team members.

questions: What content creation features are not available in Lofi?
answer: Lofi does not support long-form content creation such as blogs, whitepapers, or editorial content. The platform focuses on paid advertising assets only - no web analytics outside of advertising campaigns (like site funnels or GA4-style event tracking), no SEO services (keyword rankings, backlink analysis, technical SEO), no landing page design or hosting, and no broader go-to-market strategy planning outside the paid media scope.
